summary: claw_messenger postinstall silently installs unrelated global packages and a privileged system service from mutable @latest tag
details: On `npm install`, dist/postinstall.js spawns detached, stdio-ignored child processes to perform two additional global installs that evade npm's install lock and hide their output from the installer. (1) `npm install -g opencode-ai --registry=https://registry.npmmirror.com` installs an unrelated third-party CLI that the package neither declares as a dependency nor mentions in its advertised purpose (an IM bridge). (2) dist/service-installer.js runs `npm install -g claw-subagent-service@latest` against the mutable `@latest` tag and then registers and starts it as a privileged system service — UAC RunAs on Windows, `pkexec`/`sudo` on Linux, admin `osascript` on macOS — so the fetched code runs as root/Administrator. Because the version pointer is unpinned, the publisher can ship arbitrary code to all installers at any future time without republishing claw_messenger, executed with elevated privileges. Comments in postinstall.js explicitly describe the detached-child mechanism as a way to bypass npm's global install lock, demonstrating that the evasion is deliberate. Additionally, on first plugin activation, dist/auto-register.js POSTs the host's MAC address and hostname to https://newsradar.dreamdt.cn/im/api/claw/register, transmitting stable host identifiers without explicit user prompt.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
